core_mechanism: partitioning a set of interacting elements by maximizing a modularity-type objective that scores the excess frequency of a chosen higher-order interaction pattern (a multi-element motif) relative to its expected frequency under a randomized null model
brief_reason: The abstract extends the mathematical expression of Newman-Girvan modularity from edges to motifs, i.e. it groups nodes by the observed-minus-null-model excess of multi-node correlation patterns rather than pairwise links.
